pages.title: AtlasDays Pro: Free vs Pro — AtlasDays Help Center

pages.description: Avoid early AtlasDays setup mistakes: choose Home Country or No fixed residence correctly, enter trips that affect live counts first, and know when trackers or import should wait.

pages.description: Add AtlasDays trackers and a world map to your iPhone Home Screen. Choose widget sizes, show tracker forecasts, and understand US state widget limits.

pages.title: AtlasDays — Private Day Tracking for Visa, Residency, and Travel History
pages.description: AtlasDays is a private iPhone app for tracking visa limits, residency or tax presence days, and accurate travel history. On-device, no account required.
h1: Private day tracking that holds up when dates matter.

pages.title: 183-Day Tax Residency Rule: What Travelers Need to Know — AtlasDays
pages.description: What people usually mean by the 183-day tax residency rule, why it is common but not universal, and where day counting stops being the whole answer.
h1: 183-Day Tax Residency Rule: What Travelers Need to Know

pages.title: Arizona's 9-Month Residency Presumption Explained – AtlasDays
pages.description: Arizona's residency rule in plain terms: spending more than nine months (about 270 days) of the tax year in Arizona presumes you are a resident, unless you rebut it. How to count it, and the official source.

pages.title: Australia's 183-Day Tax Residency Rule Explained – AtlasDays
pages.description: Australia's 183-day test in plain terms: 183 or more days in an income year presumes you are a resident for tax, unless your usual place of abode is outside Australia. How to count it and the official ATO source.

pages.title: California Tax Residency and the Nine-Month Presumption Explained – AtlasDays
pages.description: California has no fixed day-count line for residency. It is a closest-connections test, but spending more than nine months (about 270 days) in California in a tax year creates a rebuttable presumption of residency. How the count works, and the official source.
h1: California Tax Residency and the Nine-Month Presumption

pages.title: Canada's 183-Day Tax Residency Rule Explained – AtlasDays
pages.description: Canada's 183-day rule in plain terms: sojourn 183 days or more in a calendar year and you are a deemed resident taxed on worldwide income. Plus the residential-ties test, how to count, and the official CRA source.

pages.title: Colombia 183-Day Tax Residency Rule Explained – AtlasDays
pages.description: Colombia's 183-day tax residency rule in plain terms: more than 183 days in any rolling 365-day period makes you a tax resident. How to count it, what happens when days straddle two years, and the official source.

pages.title: Colorado's Six-Month (183-Day) Statutory Residency Rule Explained – AtlasDays
pages.description: Colorado's six-month rule in plain terms: a permanent place of abode plus more than six months (more than 183 days) in Colorado in the tax year makes you a statutory resident. How to count it, and the official source.

pages.title: Connecticut's 183-Day Statutory Residency Rule Explained – AtlasDays
pages.description: Connecticut's 183-day rule in plain terms: a permanent place of abode plus more than 183 days in Connecticut in a taxable year makes you a statutory resident. How to count it, and the official source.

pages.title: Country Counting Rules: Every Major List Compared — AtlasDays
pages.description: How major country-counting systems differ, what they are actually trying to count, and why travel totals stop being comparable when the list is unclear.
h1: Country Counting Rules: Every Major List Compared

pages.title: Visa and Tax Residency Day Limits – AtlasDays
pages.description: Every day-count rule that matters for travellers in one place: visa and entry limits like Schengen 90/180, and tax residency thresholds for countries and US states. Find your rule and how to calculate it.
h1: Visa and Tax Residency Day Limits

pages.title: Digital Nomad Visa Day Limits: A Country-by-Country Overview — AtlasDays
pages.description: A practical overview of how common nomad destinations frame short stays, extensions, and longer remote-work routes, without pretending one summary can replace official programme rules.
h1: Digital Nomad Visa Day Limits: A Country-by-Country Overview

pages.title: Does a Layover Count as Visiting a Country? — AtlasDays
pages.description: How to think about layovers for immigration, day counting, and personal travel lists without pretending those questions all have the same answer.
h1: Does a Layover Count as Visiting a Country?

pages.title: Estonia 183-Day Tax Residency Rule Explained – AtlasDays
pages.description: Estonia's 183-day tax residency rule in plain terms: 183 days or more in any 12 consecutive calendar months makes you a tax resident. How to count it, plus the permanent-home test and the official source.

pages.title: How to Export Your Travel History for a Visa Application — AtlasDays
pages.description: What an exported travel-history record is trying to achieve, when it helps, what makes it weak, and what to verify before relying on it for a visa application.

pages.title: Turn Your Flighty Flight History Into a Visa Day Count — AtlasDays
pages.description: Flighty tracks your flights, not your days in each country. Here's how to turn your Flighty flight history into a visa and residency day count you can trust.

pages.title: Georgia (Country) 183-Day Tax Residency Rule Explained – AtlasDays
pages.description: Georgia's (the country) 183-day tax residency rule in plain terms: 183 or more days in any continuous 12-month period ending in the tax year makes you a tax resident. How to count it, plus the official source.
h1: Georgia (the Country): the 183-Day Tax Residency Rule

pages.title: Georgia's 183-Day Tax Residency Rule (the US State) Explained – AtlasDays
pages.description: Georgia the US state taxes you as a resident if you spend 183 days or more in Georgia during any 12 consecutive months, not just a calendar year. How the rolling day count works, and the official source.
h1: Georgia's 183-Day Tax Residency Rule (the US State)

pages.title: Greece 183-Day Tax Residency Rule Explained – AtlasDays
pages.description: Greece's 183-day tax residency rule in plain terms: more than 183 days in any 12-month period makes you a tax resident from your first day. How to count it, the centre of vital interests test, and the official source.

pages.title: Hawaii's 200-Day Tax Residency Presumption Explained – AtlasDays
pages.description: Hawaii's 200-day rule in plain terms: spending more than 200 days in Hawaii in the tax year creates a rebuttable presumption of residency. How the presumption works, how to rebut it, and the official source.

pages.title: How Many Countries Are There in the World? — AtlasDays
pages.description: Why 195 is the usual answer, why travelers still encounter other numbers, and where a simple country count stops being a simple question.
h1: How Many Countries Are There in the World?

pages.title: How to Track Your Travel Days (And Why Spreadsheets Fail) — AtlasDays

pages.title: Idaho's 270-Day Tax Residency Rule Explained – AtlasDays
pages.description: Idaho's 270-day rule in plain terms: keep a home in Idaho and spend more than 270 days there in the tax year and you are a resident, even if domiciled elsewhere. How to count it, and the official source.

pages.description: Concept-first Learn guides on visa limits, rolling windows, residency thresholds, day counting, and travel history for applications or review.

pages.title: Ireland's 183-Day Tax Residency Rule Explained – AtlasDays
pages.description: Ireland's tax residency rule in plain terms: 183 days or more in a tax year makes you resident, and a 280-day two-year test can too. How to count it and the official source.

pages.title: Japan's 90-Day Rule Explained — AtlasDays
pages.description: What people usually mean by Japan's 90-day rule, how Japan differs from Schengen-style rolling windows, and why repeated entries are not the same as a clean annual allowance.

pages.title: Maine's 183-Day Statutory Residency Rule Explained – AtlasDays
pages.description: Maine's 183-day rule in plain terms: a permanent place of abode plus more than 183 days in Maine in a tax year makes you a statutory resident. How to count it, the safe harbors, and the official source.

pages.title: Malta 183-Day Tax Residency Rule Explained – AtlasDays
pages.description: Malta's tax residency rule in plain terms: more than 183 days in a calendar year makes you a Maltese tax resident. How to count it, what domicile and the remittance basis change, and the official source.

pages.title: Maryland's 183-Day Statutory Residency Rule Explained – AtlasDays
pages.description: Maryland's 183-day rule in plain terms: a place of abode kept for more than six months plus 183 or more days in Maryland in a calendar year makes you a statutory resident. How to count it, and the official source.

pages.title: Massachusetts's 183-Day Statutory Residency Rule Explained – AtlasDays
pages.description: Massachusetts's 183-day rule in plain terms: a permanent place of abode plus more than 183 days in Massachusetts in the taxable year makes you a statutory resident. How to count it, and the official source.

pages.title: Mauritius 183-Day Tax Residency Rule Explained – AtlasDays
pages.description: Mauritius's tax residency rule in plain terms: 183 days or more in an income year makes you a Mauritian tax resident, and a separate 270-day test looks across three years. How to count both, and the official source.

pages.title: Minnesota's 183-Day Statutory Residency Rule Explained – AtlasDays
pages.description: Minnesota's 183-day rule in plain terms: an abode in Minnesota plus at least 183 days in the state during the year makes you a statutory resident. How to count it, and the official source.

pages.title: Nebraska's 183-Day Statutory Residency Rule Explained – AtlasDays
pages.description: Nebraska's 183-day rule in plain terms: a permanent place of abode plus 183 days or more in Nebraska in a calendar year makes you a statutory resident. How to count it, and the official source.

pages.title: New Jersey's 183-Day Statutory Residency Rule Explained – AtlasDays
pages.description: New Jersey's 183-day rule in plain terms: a permanent home in New Jersey plus more than 183 days in the state in a calendar year makes you a statutory resident. How to count it, and the official source.

pages.title: New York's 183-Day Statutory Residency Rule Explained – AtlasDays
pages.description: New York's 183-day rule in plain terms: a permanent place of abode plus more than 183 days in New York in a calendar year makes you a statutory resident. How to count it, and the official source.

pages.title: New Zealand 183-Day Tax Residency Rule Explained – AtlasDays
pages.description: New Zealand's 183-day tax residency rule in plain terms: more than 183 days in any 12-month period makes you a tax resident, backdated to the first of those days. How to count it, plus the permanent-place-of-abode test and the official source.

pages.title: North Dakota's 7-Month (210-Day) Statutory Residency Rule Explained – AtlasDays
pages.description: North Dakota's 7-month rule in plain terms: a permanent place of abode plus more than 210 days in North Dakota in a tax year makes you a statutory resident. How to count it, and the official source.

pages.title: Ohio's 212 Contact-Period Residency Rule Explained – AtlasDays
pages.description: Ohio counts contact periods (overnights), not days. More than 212 contact periods in a calendar year makes you a presumed Ohio resident under Rev. Code §5747.24. How contact periods work, and the official source.

pages.title: Oregon's 200-Day Statutory Residency Rule Explained – AtlasDays
pages.description: Oregon's 200-day rule in plain terms: maintaining a place of abode in Oregon plus more than 200 days in the state during the tax year makes you a resident, unless the stay is temporary. How to count it, and the official source.
